children.name: ValidateBuildRequest
children.purpose: Validate the build_request JSON for required fields and business rules
children.behavior: Check action is one of trigger/status/list/cancel; for trigger, ensure repo and config.build_steps present; for status/cancel, ensure build_id present; check repo+branch uniqueness for trigger

constraints.description: build_request must be a valid JSON object

acceptance_criteria.description: Valid request returns validated_request with defaults applied

children.name: RouteAction
children.purpose: Route the validated request to the appropriate action handler based on action field
children.behavior: Examine action field and call the corresponding child function: TriggerBuild, CheckBuildStatus, ListBuildHistory, or CancelBuild

constraints.description: validated_request must contain a valid action field

acceptance_criteria.description: Routes to correct handler based on action field

children.name: TriggerBuild
children.purpose: Execute a new build: create record, run compile, run test, package artifacts, update status
children.behavior: Create build record in builds store with status 'running'; run compile steps; run test steps; package artifacts; update status to 'completed' or 'failed'; store logs incrementally
children.signature: def TriggerBuild(validated_request: dict, builds: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Build records store for creating and updating build records
Artifact store for saving packaged artifacts

data_operations.description: Create build record, update status and logs
Store packaged artifacts after successful build

constraints.description: Only one build per repo+branch runs at a time

children.name: CheckBuildStatus
children.purpose: Return current status and logs for a given build_id
children.behavior: Look up build record by build_id in builds store; return status and logs
children.signature: def CheckBuildStatus(validated_request: dict, builds: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Build records store for reading build status and logs

constraints.description: build_id must exist in builds store

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns status and logs for existing build_id

children.name: ListBuildHistory
children.purpose: Return all builds filtered by repo/branch/status
children.behavior: Query builds store with optional filters from request; return list of build records
children.signature: def ListBuildHistory(validated_request: dict, builds: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Build records store for querying build history

constraints.description: Filters are optional; returns all builds if no filters provided

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of builds matching filters
Returns empty list if no builds match

children.name: CancelBuild
children.purpose: Cancel a running build: stop execution, update status to 'cancelled'
children.behavior: Look up build by build_id; if status is 'running', update status to 'cancelled' and stop any ongoing processes; if already completed, no-op
children.signature: def CancelBuild(validated_request: dict, builds: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Build records store for reading and updating build status

data_operations.description: Read build record by build_id, update status to 'cancelled' if running

constraints.description: build_id must exist in builds store
Only running builds can be cancelled

governance_notes: Parent global vars 'builds' (read_write) and 'artifacts' (read_write) are distributed as follows: 'builds' read is covered by CheckBuildStatus and ListBuildHistory; 'builds' write is covered by TriggerBuild and CancelBuild; 'artifacts' write is covered by TriggerBuild. No child requires 'artifacts' read, which is acceptable as parent only requires read_write and write is covered. All children's global_vars are subsets of parent's global_vars. Data operations and requested capabilities are consistent with global_vars assignments.
